name: odiff
description: A very fast SIMD-first image comparison library (with nodejs API)
readme_summary: Odiff is a high-performance image comparison library written in Zig, optimized with SIMD instructions for speed. It provides pixel-by-pixel difference detection for various image formats and offers both a command-line interface and Node.js bindings for integration into testing workflows.
key_features: Support for PNG, JPEG, JPG, WebP, TIFF
YIQ NTSC algorithm for visual difference
primary_use_case: Visual regression testing and identifying pixel-level differences between images, especially in automated testing pipelines.
cover_image_prompt: A meticulous digital cartographer meticulously comparing two nearly identical maps spread across a large wooden table. One map is pristine, while the other has faint, glowing red lines highlighting subtle discrepancies, like a misplaced river or a slightly altered mountain range. The cartographer uses a magnifying glass that projects a digital overlay of the differences. Small, stylized icons representing different image file formats (like JPG, PNG) are scattered around the table, and faint lines of code or data streams flow from the maps towards a small, sleek 'odiff' branded device. The scene is lit with a focused, analytical light, emphasizing precision and detail, rendered in a clean, 3D isometric illustration style.

name: CodeFormer
description: [NeurIPS 2022] Towards Robust Blind Face Restoration with Codebook Lookup Transformer
readme_summary: CodeFormer is a PyTorch-based framework for robust blind face restoration, utilizing a Codebook Lookup Transformer. It excels at enhancing degraded facial images, fixing AI-generated art, and can also perform face inpainting and colorization.
key_features: Integration with Hugging Face, Replicate, and OpenXLab for demos
primary_use_case: Restoring and enhancing the quality of degraded or low-resolution facial images.
cover_image_prompt: A skilled digital artisan, holding a magnifying glass, meticulously restoring a faded, cracked old photograph of a face. The photograph is transforming from a blurry, pixelated mess into a sharp, vibrant, and detailed portrait. Subtle glowing lines of code emanate from the artisan's tools, connecting to a small, stylized 'CodeFormer' logo hovering nearby. The background is a softly lit, modern studio with digital screens displaying before-and-after comparisons. The overall mood is one of precision and magical transformation. 3D isometric illustration style with a clean, bright color palette.

name: tegaki
description: Handwriting animation for the web. Supports any font or text.
readme_summary: Tegaki is a TypeScript library that animates any font to appear as if it's being written by hand. It requires no manual path authoring and supports integration with major web frameworks like React, Svelte, Vue, and SolidJS, as well as Vanilla JS and Web Components.
key_features: Supports React, Svelte, Vue, SolidJS, Astro, Web Components, and Vanilla JS
Interactive generator for custom font bundles
primary_use_case: Creating animated handwriting effects for text on the web, enhancing user interfaces with a dynamic and personalized touch.
cover_image_prompt: A magical quill pen, imbued with digital energy, gracefully writing text onto a scroll. The quill draws letters stroke by stroke, transforming a standard font into elegant handwriting. The input text is represented by a glowing digital blueprint, and the output is the beautifully animated script. Subtle lines of code and data particles flow around the scene, connecting the magic to technology. The background is a clean, minimalist studio with soft, ambient lighting. The overall style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a focus on clarity and a touch of whimsy, showcasing the transformation from digital font to handwritten text.

name: llm_wiki_agent
description: MCP server that turns raw sources into an interlinked Obsidian wiki with Claude Code.
readme_summary: The llm_wiki_agent is a Kotlin-based server that acts as a toolkit for an LLM (Claude Code) to ingest various raw data sources like URLs, PDFs, and audio/video files. It extracts and processes this content, transforming it into an interlinked Obsidian-compatible wiki, enabling a personal knowledge management system.
key_features: Ingests raw sources (URLs, PDFs, audio, video)
Generates interlinked wiki pages compatible with Obsidian
Manages wiki content (CRUD, search, index, log)
Communicates with LLM via MCP protocol (STDIO)
Built as an MCP server in Kotlin + Spring Boot
primary_use_case: To automatically build and maintain a personal, interlinked knowledge base (wiki) from diverse raw data sources, powered by an LLM.
cover_image_prompt: A wise old librarian, representing Claude Code, sits at a grand oak desk, surrounded by scattered scrolls (raw data like PDFs, URLs, audio tapes). With a gentle glow emanating from their hands, the librarian is meticulously weaving these scrolls into a beautifully bound, interconnected book (the Obsidian wiki). Small, ethereal threads of light connect different pages within the book, symbolizing cross-links. In the background, subtle digital particles and faint code snippets float in the air, hinting at the underlying technology. The scene is set in a cozy, well-lit study, rendered in a warm, inviting digital painting style with clear outlines and a focus on the transformation process.

name: telegram_ex
description: Elixir library for building Telegram bots with macro-based API
readme_summary: TelegramEx is an Elixir library designed for building Telegram bots. It offers a macro-based API for simplified handling of messages, callbacks, and inline queries, supporting both stateless and stateful (FSM) bot logic.
key_features: Handles messages, callbacks, and inline queries
primary_use_case: Developing Telegram bots using the Elixir programming language, with support for both simple and complex state management.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly, stylized robot assistant holding a glowing Elixir phoenix symbol, actively receiving and sorting digital messages represented as colorful envelopes. The robot is efficiently directing these envelopes into two distinct pathways: one labeled 'Stateless' with simple, direct arrows, and another labeled 'Stateful' with a winding, interactive path showing gears and memory blocks. Subtle lines of Elixir code flow around the scene, and the Telegram logo is visible on a small screen in the background. The overall mood is organized and helpful, rendered in a clean, 3D isometric illustration style with a bright, inviting color palette.

name: git-guide
readme_summary: This repository provides a comprehensive guide and cheatsheet for Git, covering everything from basic installation and commands to advanced techniques like rebasing, conflict resolution, and branching strategies. It aims to be a daily reference for developers of all skill levels.
primary_use_case: To serve as a comprehensive, easily accessible reference guide for learning and using Git version control system.
cover_image_prompt: A wise old librarian meticulously organizing a vast, intricate library of interconnected books. Each book represents a Git commit or branch, and the librarian is shown using glowing threads to connect related books, untangle complex knots (conflicts), and arrange them into clear sections. Subtle digital elements like floating code snippets and version numbers are visible in the background. The library is well-lit, with a focus on clarity and order. The overall style should be a detailed digital painting with a touch of fantasy, emphasizing the organized and comprehensive nature of Git knowledge.
